llm_answer: For non-urgent, pre-service medical and behavioral health prior authorization requests, Sunshine Health makes determinations within seven calendar days of receiving the request [6]. This timeframe may be extended by up to an additional four calendar days if Sunshine Health cannot issue a decision due to matters beyond its control [6]. For urgent medical pre-service or expedited requests, determinations are made within 48 hours of receipt [6]. llm_answer: The provided passages state that services like Durable Medical Equipment (DME)/Supplies/Orthotics & Prosthetics (O&P) and skilled therapy services are only covered for Sunshine Health members who are eligible or pending eligibility on the date the service is provided [3], [5]. Sunshine Health recommends that providers verify member eligibility before providing services. Providers can use the provider portal or the tools and resources provided by AHCA to verify eligibility [3]. It is required to verify a member’s eligibility each time a Sunshine Health member schedules an appointment and arrives for services [3]. Additionally, PCPs should verify that a member is their assigned member, as members may change PCPs and MMA plans [3].

llm_answer: You can verify member eligibility for a Sunshine Health Florida member through the Sunshine Health Provider Portal, by calling Provider Services, or by utilizing your clearinghouse.

Providers are responsible for verifying member eligibility every time a member is seen [1]. For Sunshine Health Florida members, eligibility can be checked via the Secure Web Portal (Provider.SunshineHealth.com), a 24/7 Toll-Free Interactive Voice Response (IVR) Line, or by contacting Provider Services at 1-877-687-1169 [4].
judge_reasoning: The system's response directly answers the question by providing three specific methods for verifying member eligibility, which is supported by the provided chunks.
